rationale: The cited paper’s core contribution—heterogeneous conjoint choice designs using multiple optimized subdesigns with D-error efficiency gains—directly overlaps with the citing paper’s central method of respondent-level customized conjoint designs and comparisons against Bayesian D-criteria. Given this strong methodological and domain alignment in the notes, the citation is plausibly foundational or a direct comparison point, not an obvious incidental mention. The linkage is also supported by an exact DOI match (10.1509/jmkr.42.2.210.62285).
